// Do not edit this file as it will be overwritten if HAMR codegen is rerun

//! Property normal_mode_heat -- Pre-Assert and Next-Assert VCs for every schema transition.
//! Each proof fn has an empty body: Verus discharges `requires ==> ensures`
//! via SMT; add proof hints in the body if a VC does not discharge.

#![allow(non_snake_case)]
#![allow(unused_variables)]
#![allow(unused_imports)]

use vstd::prelude::*;

use crate::assertions::*;
use crate::normal_mode_heat::assertions::*;
use crate::contracts::*;
use crate::system_state::SystemState;
use crate::write_frames::*;

verus! {

/** VC[1]: Pre-Assert -- before_oi |- OI compute + integration assumes */
pub proof fn vc_pre_assert_oi(st: SystemState)
  requires
    true /* before_oi has no assertion */,
  ensures
    true /* no assertions at out-places */,
{}

/** VC[2]: Next-Assert (task) -- before_oi + frames + OI postcondition |- after_oi */
pub proof fn vc_next_assert_task_oi(pre: SystemState, post: SystemState)
  requires
    true /* before_oi has no assertion */,
    oi_local_write_frame(pre, post),
    oi_global_write_frame(pre, post),
    oi::compute_spec_Allowed_AlarmTempWStatus_Ranges_guarantee(post.lower_alarm_tempWstatus, post.upper_alarm_tempWstatus),
    oi::compute_spec_Desired_Temps_Ordered_guarantee(post.lower_desired_tempWstatus, post.upper_desired_tempWstatus),
    oi::integration_spec_Allowed_LowerAlarmTempWstatus_guarantee(post.lower_alarm_tempWstatus),
    oi::integration_spec_Allowed_UpperAlarmTempWstatus_guarantee(post.upper_alarm_tempWstatus),
  ensures
    sys_assert_normal_mode_heat_after_oi(post),
{}

/** VC[3]: Pre-Assert -- trivial: this property does not use TS's contract (no bound out-place; contract projection) */
pub proof fn vc_pre_assert_ts(st: SystemState)
  requires
    true /* before_ts has no assertion */,
  ensures
    true /* no assertions at out-places */,
{}

/** VC[4]: Next-Assert (task) -- before_ts + frames + TS postcondition |- after_ts */
pub proof fn vc_next_assert_task_ts(pre: SystemState, post: SystemState)
  requires
    true /* before_ts has no assertion */,
    ts_local_write_frame(pre, post),
    ts_global_write_frame(pre, post),
  ensures
    true /* after_ts has no assertion */,
{}

/** VC[5]: Next-Assert (control point) -- START |- before_oi, before_ts (state unchanged) */
pub proof fn vc_next_assert_skip_t2(st: SystemState)
  requires
    true /* START has no assertion */,
  ensures
    true /* before_oi has no assertion */,
    true /* before_ts has no assertion */,
{}

/** VC[6]: Next-Assert (control point) -- after_oi, after_ts |- post_join_1 (state unchanged) */
pub proof fn vc_next_assert_skip_t3(st: SystemState)
  requires
    sys_assert_normal_mode_heat_after_oi(st),
    true /* after_ts has no assertion */,
  ensures
    sys_assert_normal_mode_heat_post_join_1(st),
{}

/** VC[7]: Pre-Assert -- before_drf |- DRF compute + integration assumes */
pub proof fn vc_pre_assert_drf(st: SystemState)
  requires
    sys_assert_normal_mode_heat_before_drf(st),
  ensures
    true /* no assertions at out-places */,
{}

/** VC[8]: Next-Assert (task) -- before_drf + frames + DRF postcondition |- after_drf */
pub proof fn vc_next_assert_task_drf(pre: SystemState, post: SystemState)
  requires
    sys_assert_normal_mode_heat_before_drf(pre),
    drf_local_write_frame(pre, post),
    drf_global_write_frame(pre, post),
  ensures
    sys_assert_normal_mode_heat_after_drf(post),
{}

/** VC[9]: Pre-Assert -- after_drf |- MRI compute + integration assumes */
pub proof fn vc_pre_assert_mri(st: SystemState)
  requires
    sys_assert_normal_mode_heat_after_drf(st),
  ensures
    mri::compute_spec_lower_is_not_higher_than_upper_assume(st.lower_desired_tempWstatus, st.upper_desired_tempWstatus),
{}

/** VC[10]: Next-Assert (task) -- after_drf + frames + MRI postcondition |- after_mri */
pub proof fn vc_next_assert_task_mri(pre: SystemState, post: SystemState)
  requires
    sys_assert_normal_mode_heat_after_drf(pre),
    mri_local_write_frame(pre, post),
    mri_global_write_frame(pre, post),
    mri::compute_spec_lower_is_lower_temp_guarantee(post.lower_desired_temp, post.upper_desired_temp),
    mri::compute_case_REQ_MRI_1(pre.regulator_mode, post.regulator_status),
    mri::compute_case_REQ_MRI_2(pre.regulator_mode, post.regulator_status),
    mri::compute_case_REQ_MRI_3(pre.regulator_mode, post.regulator_status),
    mri::compute_case_REQ_MRI_4(pre.current_tempWstatus, pre.regulator_mode, post.displayed_temp),
    mri::compute_case_REQ_MRI_5(),
    mri::compute_case_REQ_MRI_6(pre.upper_desired_tempWstatus, post.reg_interface_failure),
    mri::compute_case_REQ_MRI_7(pre.lower_desired_tempWstatus, pre.upper_desired_tempWstatus, post.reg_interface_failure),
    mri::compute_case_REQ_MRI_8(pre.lower_desired_tempWstatus, pre.upper_desired_tempWstatus, post.reg_interface_failure, post.lower_desired_temp, post.upper_desired_temp),
    mri::compute_case_REQ_MRI_9(post.reg_interface_failure),
  ensures
    sys_assert_normal_mode_heat_after_mri(post),
{}

/** VC[11]: Pre-Assert -- after_mri |- MRM compute + integration assumes */
pub proof fn vc_pre_assert_mrm(st: SystemState)
  requires
    sys_assert_normal_mode_heat_after_mri(st),
  ensures
    true /* no assertions at out-places */,
{}

/** VC[12]: Next-Assert (task) -- after_mri + frames + MRM postcondition |- after_mrm */
pub proof fn vc_next_assert_task_mrm(pre: SystemState, post: SystemState)
  requires
    sys_assert_normal_mode_heat_after_mri(pre),
    mrm_local_write_frame(pre, post),
    mrm_global_write_frame(pre, post),
    mrm::compute_spec_update_lastRegulatorMode_guarantee(post.reg_last_mode, post.regulator_mode),
    mrm::compute_case_REQ_MRM_2(post.reg_last_mode, pre.current_tempWstatus, pre.reg_interface_failure, pre.internal_failure, post.regulator_mode),
    mrm::compute_case_REQ_MRM_Maintain_Normal(post.reg_last_mode, pre.current_tempWstatus, pre.reg_interface_failure, pre.internal_failure, post.regulator_mode),
    mrm::compute_case_REQ_MRM_3(post.reg_last_mode, pre.current_tempWstatus, pre.reg_interface_failure, pre.internal_failure, post.regulator_mode),
    mrm::compute_case_REQ_MRM_4(post.reg_last_mode, post.regulator_mode),
    mrm::compute_case_REQ_MRM_MaintainFailed(post.reg_last_mode, post.regulator_mode),
  ensures
    sys_assert_normal_mode_heat_after_mrm(post),
{}

/** VC[13]: Pre-Assert -- after_mrm |- MHS compute + integration assumes */
pub proof fn vc_pre_assert_mhs(st: SystemState)
  requires
    sys_assert_normal_mode_heat_after_mrm(st),
  ensures
    mhs::compute_spec_lower_is_lower_temp_assume(st.lower_desired_temp, st.upper_desired_temp),
{}

/** VC[14]: Next-Assert (task) -- after_mrm + frames + MHS postcondition |- after_mhs */
pub proof fn vc_next_assert_task_mhs(pre: SystemState, post: SystemState)
  requires
    sys_assert_normal_mode_heat_after_mrm(pre),
    mhs_local_write_frame(pre, post),
    mhs_global_write_frame(pre, post),
    mhs::compute_spec_lastCmd_guarantee(post.lastCmd, post.heat_control),
    mhs::compute_case_REQ_MHS_1(pre.regulator_mode, post.heat_control),
    mhs::compute_case_REQ_MHS_2(pre.current_tempWstatus, pre.lower_desired_temp, pre.regulator_mode, post.heat_control),
    mhs::compute_case_REQ_MHS_3(pre.current_tempWstatus, pre.regulator_mode, pre.upper_desired_temp, post.heat_control),
    mhs::compute_case_REQ_MHS_4(pre.lastCmd, pre.current_tempWstatus, pre.lower_desired_temp, pre.regulator_mode, pre.upper_desired_temp, post.heat_control),
    mhs::compute_case_REQ_MHS_5(pre.regulator_mode, post.heat_control),
  ensures
    sys_assert_normal_mode_heat_after_mhs(post),
{}

/** VC[15]: Pre-Assert -- trivial: this property does not use DMF's contract (no bound out-place; contract projection) */
pub proof fn vc_pre_assert_dmf(st: SystemState)
  requires
    true /* before_dmf has no assertion */,
  ensures
    true /* no assertions at out-places */,
{}

/** VC[16]: Next-Assert (task) -- before_dmf + frames + DMF postcondition |- after_dmf */
pub proof fn vc_next_assert_task_dmf(pre: SystemState, post: SystemState)
  requires
    true /* before_dmf has no assertion */,
    dmf_local_write_frame(pre, post),
    dmf_global_write_frame(pre, post),
  ensures
    true /* after_dmf has no assertion */,
{}

/** VC[17]: Pre-Assert -- trivial: this property does not use MMI's contract (no bound out-place; contract projection) */
pub proof fn vc_pre_assert_mmi(st: SystemState)
  requires
    true /* after_dmf has no assertion */,
  ensures
    true /* no assertions at out-places */,
{}

/** VC[18]: Next-Assert (task) -- after_dmf + frames + MMI postcondition |- after_mmi */
pub proof fn vc_next_assert_task_mmi(pre: SystemState, post: SystemState)
  requires
    true /* after_dmf has no assertion */,
    mmi_local_write_frame(pre, post),
    mmi_global_write_frame(pre, post),
  ensures
    true /* after_mmi has no assertion */,
{}

/** VC[19]: Pre-Assert -- trivial: this property does not use MMM's contract (no bound out-place; contract projection) */
pub proof fn vc_pre_assert_mmm(st: SystemState)
  requires
    true /* after_mmi has no assertion */,
  ensures
    true /* no assertions at out-places */,
{}

/** VC[20]: Next-Assert (task) -- after_mmi + frames + MMM postcondition |- after_mmm */
pub proof fn vc_next_assert_task_mmm(pre: SystemState, post: SystemState)
  requires
    true /* after_mmi has no assertion */,
    mmm_local_write_frame(pre, post),
    mmm_global_write_frame(pre, post),
  ensures
    true /* after_mmm has no assertion */,
{}

/** VC[21]: Pre-Assert -- trivial: this property does not use MA's contract (no bound out-place; contract projection) */
pub proof fn vc_pre_assert_ma(st: SystemState)
  requires
    true /* after_mmm has no assertion */,

{}

/** VC[22]: Next-Assert (task) -- after_mmm + frames + MA postcondition |- after_ma */

  requires
    true /* after_mmm has no assertion */,

    ma_global_write_frame(pre, post),
  ensures
    true /* after_ma has no assertion */,
{}

/** VC[23]: Next-Assert (control point) -- post_join_1 |- before_drf, before_dmf (state unchanged) */
pub proof fn vc_next_assert_skip_t12(st: SystemState)
  requires
    sys_assert_normal_mode_heat_post_join_1(st),
  ensures
    sys_assert_normal_mode_heat_before_drf(st),
    true /* before_dmf has no assertion */,
{}

/** VC[24]: Next-Assert (control point) -- after_mhs, after_ma |- post_join_2 (state unchanged) */
pub proof fn vc_next_assert_skip_t13(st: SystemState)
  requires
    sys_assert_normal_mode_heat_after_mhs(st),
    true /* after_ma has no assertion */,

    true /* post_join_2 has no assertion */,

/** VC[25]: Pre-Assert -- trivial: this property does not use HS's contract (no bound out-place; contract projection) */
pub proof fn vc_pre_assert_hs(st: SystemState)
  requires
    true /* post_join_2 has no assertion */,

    true /* no assertions at out-places */,
{}

/** VC[26]: Next-Assert (task) -- post_join_2 + frames + HS postcondition |- after_hs */
pub proof fn vc_next_assert_task_hs(pre: SystemState, post: SystemState)

/** VC[27]: Next-Assert (control point) -- after_hs |- END (state unchanged) */
pub proof fn vc_next_assert_skip_t15(st: SystemState)

    true /* after_hs has no assertion */,
  ensures
    true /* END has no assertion */,
{}

//! Property normal_mode_heat -- Non-Blocking and Preservation VCs for every

/** VC[29]: Non-Blocking -- OI firing does not block TS (MHIP pair t0/t1) */
pub proof fn vc_non_blocking_oi_ts(pre: SystemState, post: SystemState)
  requires
    true /* before_oi has no assertion */,
    true /* before_ts has no assertion */,
    oi_global_write_frame(pre, post),
  ensures
    true /* before_ts has no assertion */,
{}

/** VC[30]: Non-Blocking -- TS firing does not block OI (MHIP pair t0/t1) */
pub proof fn vc_non_blocking_ts_oi(pre: SystemState, post: SystemState)
  requires
    true /* before_oi has no assertion */,
    true /* before_ts has no assertion */,
    ts_global_write_frame(pre, post),
  ensures
    true /* before_oi has no assertion */,
{}

/** VC[31]: Preservation -- OI firing preserves TS's post-assertions (MHIP pair t0/t1) */
pub proof fn vc_preservation_oi_ts(pre: SystemState, post: SystemState)
  requires
    true /* before_oi has no assertion */,
    true /* after_ts has no assertion */,
    oi_global_write_frame(pre, post),
  ensures
    true /* after_ts has no assertion */,
{}

/** VC[32]: Preservation -- TS firing preserves OI's post-assertions (MHIP pair t0/t1) */
pub proof fn vc_preservation_ts_oi(pre: SystemState, post: SystemState)

    true /* before_ts has no assertion */,
    sys_assert_normal_mode_heat_after_oi(pre),
    ts_global_write_frame(pre, post),
  ensures
    sys_assert_normal_mode_heat_after_oi(post),
{}

    oi::compute_spec_Desired_Temps_Ordered_guarantee(post.lower_desired_tempWstatus, post.upper_desired_tempWstatus),
    oi::integration_spec_Allowed_LowerAlarmTempWstatus_guarantee(post.lower_alarm_tempWstatus),

    mmm::compute_case_REQ_MMM_3(post.lastMonitorMode, pre.current_tempWstatus, pre.mon_interface_failure, pre.mon_internal_failure, post.monitor_mode),
    mmm::compute_case_REQ_MMM_4(post.lastMonitorMode, post.monitor_mode),
    mmm::compute_case_Failed_Mode_Absorbing(post.lastMonitorMode, post.monitor_mode),
  ensures

  ensures
    ma::compute_spec_Figure_A_7_assume(st.lower_alarm_temp, st.monitor_mode, st.upper_alarm_temp),
    ma::compute_spec_Table_A_12_LowerAlarmTemp_assume(st.lower_alarm_temp, st.monitor_mode),

{}

    ma_local_write_frame(pre, post),

    ma::compute_case_REQ_MA_1(post.ma_lastCmd, pre.monitor_mode, post.alarm_control),

    ma::compute_case_REQ_MA_5(post.ma_lastCmd, pre.monitor_mode, post.alarm_control),

// Do not edit this file as it will be overwritten if HAMR codegen is rerun

//! Property regulator_failsafe_latching -- Pre-Assert and Next-Assert VCs for every schema transition.
//! Each proof fn has an empty body: Verus discharges `requires ==> ensures`
//! via SMT; add proof hints in the body if a VC does not discharge.

#![allow(non_snake_case)]
#![allow(unused_variables)]
#![allow(unused_imports)]

use vstd::prelude::*;

use crate::assertions::*;
use crate::regulator_failsafe_latching::assertions::*;
use crate::contracts::*;
use crate::system_state::SystemState;
use crate::write_frames::*;

verus! {

/** VC[1]: Pre-Assert -- before_oi |- OI compute + integration assumes */
pub proof fn vc_pre_assert_oi(st: SystemState)
  requires
    true /* before_oi has no assertion */,
  ensures
    true /* no assertions at out-places */,
{}

/** VC[2]: Next-Assert (task) -- before_oi + frames + OI postcondition |- after_oi */
pub proof fn vc_next_assert_task_oi(pre: SystemState, post: SystemState)
  requires
    true /* before_oi has no assertion */,
    oi_local_write_frame(pre, post),
    oi_global_write_frame(pre, post),
    oi::compute_spec_Allowed_AlarmTempWStatus_Ranges_guarantee(post.lower_alarm_tempWstatus, post.upper_alarm_tempWstatus),
    oi::compute_spec_Desired_Temps_Ordered_guarantee(post.lower_desired_tempWstatus, post.upper_desired_tempWstatus),
    oi::integration_spec_Allowed_LowerAlarmTempWstatus_guarantee(post.lower_alarm_tempWstatus),
    oi::integration_spec_Allowed_UpperAlarmTempWstatus_guarantee(post.upper_alarm_tempWstatus),
  ensures

//! Copies of the component GUMBO contracts for system-level verification.
//! The originals live in the component crates, which this crate cannot
//! depend on (they are staticlibs); both are generated from the same
//! GUMBO source, so they are identical by construction (TCB item T3).

// -- Isolette_Single_Sensor_Instance.thermostat.rt.mri.mri --
pub mod mri {
  use super::*;

  // ---- GUMBO subclause functions ----

  pub open spec fn ROUND(num: i32) -> i32
  {
    num
  }

  /** initialize guarantee RegulatorStatusIsInitiallyInit
    */
  pub open spec fn initialize_RegulatorStatusIsInitiallyInit(
    api_regulator_status: Isolette_Data_Model::Status) -> bool
  {
    api_regulator_status == Isolette_Data_Model::Status::Init_Status
  }

  /** compute assume lower_is_not_higher_than_upper
    *   The ordering of the desired temperature range is only a meaningful
    *   constraint on Valid readings
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_spec_lower_is_not_higher_than_upper_assume(
    api_lower_desired_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i,
    api_upper_desired_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i) -> bool
  {
    ((api_lower_desired_tempWstatus.status == Isolette_Data_Model::ValueStatus::Valid) &&
      (api_upper_desired_tempWstatus.status == Isolette_Data_Model::ValueStatus::Valid)) ==>
      (api_lower_desired_tempWstatus.degrees <= api_upper_desired_tempWstatus.degrees)
  }

  /** compute guarantee lower_is_lower_temp
    *   Derived requirement, not in AR-08-32: MHS unconditionally assumes the
    *   Desired Range is well-ordered,.
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_spec_lower_is_lower_temp_guarantee(
    api_lower_desired_temp: Isolette_Data_Model::Temp_i,
    api_upper_desired_temp: Isolette_Data_Model::Temp_i) -> bool
  {
    api_lower_desired_temp.degrees <= api_upper_desired_temp.degrees
  }

  /** compute case REQ_MRI_1
    *   If the Regulator Mode is INIT,
    *   the Regulator Status shall be set to Init.
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=107 
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_case_REQ_MRI_1(
    api_regulator_mode: Isolette_Data_Model::Regulator_Mode,
    api_regulator_status: Isolette_Data_Model::Status) -> bool
  {
    (api_regulator_mode == Isolette_Data_Model::Regulator_Mode::Init_Regulator_Mode)
    ==> (api_regulator_status == Isolette_Data_Model::Status::Init_Status)
  }

  /** compute case REQ_MRI_2
    *   If the Regulator Mode is NORMAL,
    *   the Regulator Status shall be set to On
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=107 
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_case_REQ_MRI_2(
    api_regulator_mode: Isolette_Data_Model::Regulator_Mode,
    api_regulator_status: Isolette_Data_Model::Status) -> bool
  {
    (api_regulator_mode == Isolette_Data_Model::Regulator_Mode::Normal_Regulator_Mode)
    ==> (api_regulator_status == Isolette_Data_Model::Status::On_Status)
  }

  /** compute case REQ_MRI_3
    *   If the Regulator Mode is FAILED,
    *   the Regulator Status shall be set to Failed.
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=107 
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_case_REQ_MRI_3(
    api_regulator_mode: Isolette_Data_Model::Regulator_Mode,
    api_regulator_status: Isolette_Data_Model::Status) -> bool
  {
    (api_regulator_mode == Isolette_Data_Model::Regulator_Mode::Failed_Regulator_Mode)
    ==> (api_regulator_status == Isolette_Data_Model::Status::Failed_Status)
  }

  /** compute case REQ_MRI_4
    *   If the Regulator Mode is NORMAL, the
    *   Display Temperature shall be set to the value of the
    *   Current Temperature rounded to the nearest integer.
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=108 
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_case_REQ_MRI_4(
    api_current_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i,
    api_regulator_mode: Isolette_Data_Model::Regulator_Mode,
    api_displayed_temp: Isolette_Data_Model::Temp_i) -> bool
  {
    (api_regulator_mode == Isolette_Data_Model::Regulator_Mode::Normal_Regulator_Mode)
    ==> (api_displayed_temp.degrees == ROUND(api_current_tempWstatus.degrees))
  }

  /** compute case REQ_MRI_5
    *   If the Regulator Mode is not NORMAL,
    *   the value of the Display Temperature is UNSPECIFIED.
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=108 
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_case_REQ_MRI_5() -> bool
  {
    true
  }

  /** compute case REQ_MRI_6
    *   If the Status attribute of the Lower Desired Temperature
    *   or the Upper Desired Temperature is Invalid,
    *   the Regulator Interface Failure shall be set to True.
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=108 
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_case_REQ_MRI_6(
    api_upper_desired_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i,
    api_interface_failure: Isolette_Data_Model::Failure_Flag_i) -> bool
  {
    ((api_upper_desired_tempWstatus.status != Isolette_Data_Model::ValueStatus::Valid) ||
      (api_upper_desired_tempWstatus.status != Isolette_Data_Model::ValueStatus::Valid))
    ==> (api_interface_failure.flag)
  }

  /** compute case REQ_MRI_7
    *   If the Status attribute of the Lower Desired Temperature
    *   and the Upper Desired Temperature is Valid,
    *   the Regulator Interface Failure shall be set to False.
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=108 
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_case_REQ_MRI_7(
    api_lower_desired_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i,
    api_upper_desired_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i,
    api_interface_failure: Isolette_Data_Model::Failure_Flag_i) -> bool
  {
    api_interface_failure.flag == !((api_upper_desired_tempWstatus.status == Isolette_Data_Model::ValueStatus::Valid) &&
      (api_lower_desired_tempWstatus.status == Isolette_Data_Model::ValueStatus::Valid))
  }

  /** compute case REQ_MRI_8
    *   If the Regulator Interface Failure is False,
    *   the Desired Range shall be set to the Desired Temperature Range.
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=108 
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_case_REQ_MRI_8(
    api_lower_desired_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i,
    api_upper_desired_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i,
    api_interface_failure: Isolette_Data_Model::Failure_Flag_i,
    api_lower_desired_temp: Isolette_Data_Model::Temp_i,
    api_upper_desired_temp: Isolette_Data_Model::Temp_i) -> bool
  {
    !(api_interface_failure.flag) ==>
      ((api_lower_desired_temp.degrees == api_lower_desired_tempWstatus.degrees) &&
        (api_upper_desired_temp.degrees == api_upper_desired_tempWstatus.degrees))
  }

  /** compute case REQ_MRI_9
    *   If the Regulator Interface Failure is True,
    *   the Desired Range is UNSPECIFIED.
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=108 
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_case_REQ_MRI_9(
    api_interface_failure: Isolette_Data_Model::Failure_Flag_i) -> bool
  {
    api_interface_failure.flag ==> true
  }
}

// -- Isolette_Single_Sensor_Instance.thermostat.rt.mhs.mhs --
pub mod mhs {
  use super::*;

  /** initialize guarantee initlastCmd
    */
  pub open spec fn initialize_initlastCmd(
    lastCmd: Isolette_Data_Model::On_Off) -> bool
  {
    lastCmd == Isolette_Data_Model::On_Off::Off
  }

  /** initialize guarantee REQ_MHS_1
    *   If the Regulator Mode is INIT, the Heat Control shall be
    *   set to Off.
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=110 
    */
  pub open spec fn initialize_REQ_MHS_1(
    api_heat_control: Isolette_Data_Model::On_Off) -> bool
  {
    api_heat_control == Isolette_Data_Model::On_Off::Off
  }

  /** compute assume lower_is_lower_temp
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_spec_lower_is_lower_temp_assume(
    api_lower_desired_temp: Isolette_Data_Model::Temp_i,
    api_upper_desired_temp: Isolette_Data_Model::Temp_i) -> bool
  {
    api_lower_desired_temp.degrees <= api_upper_desired_temp.degrees
  }

  /** compute guarantee lastCmd
    *   Set lastCmd to value of output Cmd port
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_spec_lastCmd_guarantee(
    lastCmd: Isolette_Data_Model::On_Off,
    api_heat_control: Isolette_Data_Model::On_Off) -> bool
  {
    lastCmd == api_heat_control
  }

  /** compute case REQ_MHS_1
    *   If the Regulator Mode is INIT, the Heat Control shall be
    *   set to Off.
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=110 
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_case_REQ_MHS_1(
    api_regulator_mode: Isolette_Data_Model::Regulator_Mode,
    api_heat_control: Isolette_Data_Model::On_Off) -> bool
  {
    (api_regulator_mode == Isolette_Data_Model::Regulator_Mode::Init_Regulator_Mode)
    ==> (api_heat_control == Isolette_Data_Model::On_Off::Off)
  }

  /** compute case REQ_MHS_2
    *   If the Regulator Mode is NORMAL and the Current Temperature is less than
    *   the Lower Desired Temperature, the Heat Control shall be set to On.
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=110 
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_case_REQ_MHS_2(
    api_current_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i,
    api_lower_desired_temp: Isolette_Data_Model::Temp_i,
    api_regulator_mode: Isolette_Data_Model::Regulator_Mode,
    api_heat_control: Isolette_Data_Model::On_Off) -> bool
  {
    ((api_regulator_mode == Isolette_Data_Model::Regulator_Mode::Normal_Regulator_Mode) &&
      (api_current_tempWstatus.degrees < api_lower_desired_temp.degrees))
    ==> (api_heat_control == Isolette_Data_Model::On_Off::Onn)
  }

  /** compute case REQ_MHS_3
    *   If the Regulator Mode is NORMAL and the Current Temperature is greater than
    *   the Upper Desired Temperature, the Heat Control shall be set to Off.
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=110 
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_case_REQ_MHS_3(
    api_current_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i,
    api_regulator_mode: Isolette_Data_Model::Regulator_Mode,
    api_upper_desired_temp: Isolette_Data_Model::Temp_i,
    api_heat_control: Isolette_Data_Model::On_Off) -> bool
  {
    ((api_regulator_mode == Isolette_Data_Model::Regulator_Mode::Normal_Regulator_Mode) &&
      (api_current_tempWstatus.degrees > api_upper_desired_temp.degrees))
    ==> (api_heat_control == Isolette_Data_Model::On_Off::Off)
  }

  /** compute case REQ_MHS_4
    *   If the Regulator Mode is NORMAL and the Current
    *   Temperature is greater than or equal to the Lower Desired Temperature
    *   and less than or equal to the Upper Desired Temperature, the value of
    *   the Heat Control shall not be changed.
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=110 
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_case_REQ_MHS_4(
    In_lastCmd: Isolette_Data_Model::On_Off,
    api_current_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i,
    api_lower_desired_temp: Isolette_Data_Model::Temp_i,
    api_regulator_mode: Isolette_Data_Model::Regulator_Mode,
    api_upper_desired_temp: Isolette_Data_Model::Temp_i,
    api_heat_control: Isolette_Data_Model::On_Off) -> bool
  {
    ((api_regulator_mode == Isolette_Data_Model::Regulator_Mode::Normal_Regulator_Mode) &&
      ((api_current_tempWstatus.degrees >= api_lower_desired_temp.degrees) &&
        (api_current_tempWstatus.degrees <= api_upper_desired_temp.degrees)))
    ==> (api_heat_control == In_lastCmd)
  }

  /** compute case REQ_MHS_5
    *   If the Regulator Mode is FAILED, the Heat Control shall be
    *   set to Off.
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=111 
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_case_REQ_MHS_5(
    api_regulator_mode: Isolette_Data_Model::Regulator_Mode,
    api_heat_control: Isolette_Data_Model::On_Off) -> bool
  {
    (api_regulator_mode == Isolette_Data_Model::Regulator_Mode::Failed_Regulator_Mode)
    ==> (api_heat_control == Isolette_Data_Model::On_Off::Off)
  }
}

// -- Isolette_Single_Sensor_Instance.thermostat.rt.mrm.mrm --
pub mod mrm {
  use super::*;

  // ---- GUMBO subclause functions ----

  pub open spec fn regulator_status(
    interface_failure: Isolette_Data_Model::Failure_Flag_i,
    internal_failure: Isolette_Data_Model::Failure_Flag_i,
    current_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i) -> bool
  {
    !(interface_failure.flag || internal_failure.flag) &&
      (current_tempWstatus.status == Isolette_Data_Model::ValueStatus::Valid)
  }

  pub open spec fn timeout_condition_satisfied() -> bool
  {
    false
  }

  /** initialize guarantee REQ_MRM_1
    *   The initial mode of the regular is INIT
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=109 
    */
  pub open spec fn initialize_REQ_MRM_1(
    api_regulator_mode: Isolette_Data_Model::Regulator_Mode) -> bool
  {
    api_regulator_mode == Isolette_Data_Model::Regulator_Mode::Init_Regulator_Mode
  }

  /** compute guarantee update_lastRegulatorMode
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_spec_update_lastRegulatorMode_guarantee(
    lastRegulatorMode: Isolette_Data_Model::Regulator_Mode,
    api_regulator_mode: Isolette_Data_Model::Regulator_Mode) -> bool
  {
    lastRegulatorMode == api_regulator_mode
  }

  /** compute case REQ_MRM_2
    *   'transition from Init to Normal'
    *   If the current regulator mode is Init, then
    *   the regulator mode is set to NORMAL iff the regulator status is valid (see Table A-10), i.e.,
    *     if NOT (Regulator Interface Failure OR Regulator Internal Failure)
    *        AND Current Temperature.Status = Valid
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=109 
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_case_REQ_MRM_2(
    lastRegulatorMode: Isolette_Data_Model::Regulator_Mode,
    api_current_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i,
    api_interface_failure: Isolette_Data_Model::Failure_Flag_i,
    api_internal_failure: Isolette_Data_Model::Failure_Flag_i,
    api_regulator_mode: Isolette_Data_Model::Regulator_Mode) -> bool
  {
    (lastRegulatorMode == Isolette_Data_Model::Regulator_Mode::Init_Regulator_Mode)
    ==> ((regulator_status(api_interface_failure, api_internal_failure, api_current_tempWstatus) && !(timeout_condition_satisfied())) == (api_regulator_mode == Isolette_Data_Model::Regulator_Mode::Normal_Regulator_Mode))
  }

  /** compute case REQ_MRM_Maintain_Normal
    *   'maintaining NORMAL, NORMAL to NORMAL'
    *   If the current regulator mode is Normal, then
    *   the regulator mode is stays normal iff
    *   the regulaor status is not false i.e.,
    *          if NOT(
    *              (Regulator Interface Failure OR Regulator Internal Failure)
    *              OR NOT(Current Temperature.Status = Valid)
    *          )
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=109 
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_case_REQ_MRM_Maintain_Normal(
    lastRegulatorMode: Isolette_Data_Model::Regulator_Mode,
    api_current_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i,
    api_interface_failure: Isolette_Data_Model::Failure_Flag_i,
    api_internal_failure: Isolette_Data_Model::Failure_Flag_i,
    api_regulator_mode: Isolette_Data_Model::Regulator_Mode) -> bool
  {
    ((lastRegulatorMode == Isolette_Data_Model::Regulator_Mode::Normal_Regulator_Mode) &&
      regulator_status(api_interface_failure, api_internal_failure, api_current_tempWstatus))
    ==> (api_regulator_mode == Isolette_Data_Model::Regulator_Mode::Normal_Regulator_Mode)
  }

  /** compute case REQ_MRM_3
    *   'transition for NORMAL to FAILED'
    *   If the current regulator mode is Normal, then
    *   the regulator mode is set to Failed iff
    *   the regulator status is false, i.e.,
    *      if  (Regulator Interface Failure OR Regulator Internal Failure)
    *          OR NOT(Current Temperature.Status = Valid)
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=109 
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_case_REQ_MRM_3(
    lastRegulatorMode: Isolette_Data_Model::Regulator_Mode,
    api_current_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i,
    api_interface_failure: Isolette_Data_Model::Failure_Flag_i,
    api_internal_failure: Isolette_Data_Model::Failure_Flag_i,
    api_regulator_mode: Isolette_Data_Model::Regulator_Mode) -> bool
  {
    (lastRegulatorMode == Isolette_Data_Model::Regulator_Mode::Normal_Regulator_Mode)
    ==> (!(regulator_status(api_interface_failure, api_internal_failure, api_current_tempWstatus)) == (api_regulator_mode == Isolette_Data_Model::Regulator_Mode::Failed_Regulator_Mode))
  }

  /** compute case REQ_MRM_4
    *   'transition from INIT to FAILED'
    *   If the current regulator mode is Init, then the regulator mode is set to
    *   Failed iff the time during which the thread has been in Init mode exceeds the
    *   Regulator Init Timeout value (parallel to REQ-MMM-4, the monitor's Init timeout).
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=109
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_case_REQ_MRM_4(
    lastRegulatorMode: Isolette_Data_Model::Regulator_Mode,
    api_regulator_mode: Isolette_Data_Model::Regulator_Mode) -> bool
  {
    (lastRegulatorMode == Isolette_Data_Model::Regulator_Mode::Init_Regulator_Mode)
    ==> (timeout_condition_satisfied() == (api_regulator_mode == Isolette_Data_Model::Regulator_Mode::Failed_Regulator_Mode))
  }

  /** compute case REQ_MRM_MaintainFailed
    *   'maintaining FAIL, FAIL to FAIL'
    *   If the current regulator mode is Failed, then
    *   the regulator mode remains in the Failed state and the LastRegulator mode remains Failed.REQ-MRM-Maintain-Failed
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=109
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_case_REQ_MRM_MaintainFailed(
    lastRegulatorMode: Isolette_Data_Model::Regulator_Mode,
    api_regulator_mode: Isolette_Data_Model::Regulator_Mode) -> bool
  {
    (lastRegulatorMode == Isolette_Data_Model::Regulator_Mode::Failed_Regulator_Mode)
    ==> (api_regulator_mode == Isolette_Data_Model::Regulator_Mode::Failed_Regulator_Mode)
  }
}

// -- Isolette_Single_Sensor_Instance.thermostat.mt.mmi.mmi --
pub mod mmi {
  use super::*;

  // ---- GUMBO subclause functions ----

  pub open spec fn timeout_condition_satisfied() -> bool
  {
    true
  }

  /** initialize guarantee monitorStatusInitiallyInit
    */
  pub open spec fn initialize_monitorStatusInitiallyInit(
    api_monitor_status: Isolette_Data_Model::Status) -> bool
  {
    api_monitor_status == Isolette_Data_Model::Status::Init_Status
  }

  /** compute assume Allowed_AlarmTempWstatus_Ranges
    *   An integration constraint can only refer to a single port, so need a general assume clause
    *   in order to relate the lower and uper temps
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_spec_Allowed_AlarmTempWstatus_Ranges_assume(
    api_lower_alarm_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i,
    api_upper_alarm_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i) -> bool
  {
    GUMBO_Library::Allowed_AlarmTempWStatus_Ranges_spec(api_lower_alarm_tempWstatus, api_upper_alarm_tempWstatus)
  }

  /** compute case REQ_MMI_1
    *   If the Manage Monitor Interface mode is INIT,
    *   the Monitor Status shall be set to Init.
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=113 
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_case_REQ_MMI_1(
    api_monitor_mode: Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode,
    api_monitor_status: Isolette_Data_Model::Status) -> bool
  {
    (api_monitor_mode == Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode::Init_Monitor_Mode)
    ==> (api_monitor_status == Isolette_Data_Model::Status::Init_Status)
  }

  /** compute case REQ_MMI_2
    *   If the Manage Monitor Interface mode is NORMAL,
    *   the Monitor Status shall be set to On
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=113 
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_case_REQ_MMI_2(
    api_monitor_mode: Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode,
    api_monitor_status: Isolette_Data_Model::Status) -> bool
  {
    (api_monitor_mode == Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode::Normal_Monitor_Mode)
    ==> (api_monitor_status == Isolette_Data_Model::Status::On_Status)
  }

  /** compute case REQ_MMI_3
    *   If the Manage Monitor Interface mode is FAILED,
    *   the Monitor Status shall be set to Failed.
    *   Latency: < Max Operator Response Time
    *   Tolerance: N/A
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=113 
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_case_REQ_MMI_3(
    api_monitor_mode: Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode,
    api_monitor_status: Isolette_Data_Model::Status) -> bool
  {
    (api_monitor_mode == Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode::Failed_Monitor_Mode)
    ==> (api_monitor_status == Isolette_Data_Model::Status::Failed_Status)
  }

  /** compute case REQ_MMI_4
    *   If the Status attribute of the Lower Alarm Temperature
    *   or the Upper Alarm Temperature is Invalid,
    *   the Monitor Interface Failure shall be set to True
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=113 
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_case_REQ_MMI_4(
    api_lower_alarm_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i,
    api_upper_alarm_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i,
    api_interface_failure: Isolette_Data_Model::Failure_Flag_i) -> bool
  {
    ((api_lower_alarm_tempWstatus.status == Isolette_Data_Model::ValueStatus::Invalid) ||
      (api_upper_alarm_tempWstatus.status == Isolette_Data_Model::ValueStatus::Invalid))
    ==> (api_interface_failure.flag)
  }

  /** compute case REQ_MMI_5
    *   If the Status attribute of the Lower Alarm Temperature
    *   and the Upper Alarm Temperature is Valid,
    *   the Monitor Interface Failure shall be set to False
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=113 
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_case_REQ_MMI_5(
    api_lower_alarm_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i,
    api_upper_alarm_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i,
    api_interface_failure: Isolette_Data_Model::Failure_Flag_i) -> bool
  {
    ((api_lower_alarm_tempWstatus.status == Isolette_Data_Model::ValueStatus::Valid) &&
      (api_upper_alarm_tempWstatus.status == Isolette_Data_Model::ValueStatus::Valid))
    ==> (!(api_interface_failure.flag))
  }

  /** compute case REQ_MMI_6
    *   If the Monitor Interface Failure is False,
    *   the Alarm Range variable shall be set to the Desired Temperature Range
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=113 
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_case_REQ_MMI_6(
    api_lower_alarm_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i,
    api_upper_alarm_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i,
    api_interface_failure: Isolette_Data_Model::Failure_Flag_i,
    api_lower_alarm_temp: Isolette_Data_Model::Temp_i,
    api_upper_alarm_temp: Isolette_Data_Model::Temp_i) -> bool
  {
    !(api_interface_failure.flag) ==>
      ((api_lower_alarm_temp.degrees == api_lower_alarm_tempWstatus.degrees) &&
        (api_upper_alarm_temp.degrees == api_upper_alarm_tempWstatus.degrees))
  }

  /** compute case REQ_MMI_7
    *   If the Monitor Interface Failure is True,
    *   the Alarm Range variable is UNSPECIFIED
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=113 
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_case_REQ_MMI_7(
    api_interface_failure: Isolette_Data_Model::Failure_Flag_i) -> bool
  {
    api_interface_failure.flag ==> true
  }

  /** integration assume Allowed_UpperAlarmTemp (I-Assm, incoming port upper_alarm_tempWstatus)
    */
  pub open spec fn integration_spec_Allowed_UpperAlarmTemp_assume(
    api_upper_alarm_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i) -> bool
  {
    GUMBO_Library::Allowed_UpperAlarmTempWStatus_spec(api_upper_alarm_tempWstatus)
  }

  /** integration assume Allowed_LowerAlarmTemp (I-Assm, incoming port lower_alarm_tempWstatus)
    */
  pub open spec fn integration_spec_Allowed_LowerAlarmTemp_assume(
    api_lower_alarm_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i) -> bool
  {
    GUMBO_Library::Allowed_LowerAlarmTempWStatus_spec(api_lower_alarm_tempWstatus)
  }
}

// -- Isolette_Single_Sensor_Instance.thermostat.mt.ma.ma --
pub mod ma {
  use super::*;

  // ---- GUMBO subclause functions ----

  pub open spec fn timeout_condition_satisfied() -> bool
  {
    true
  }

  /** initialize guarantee REQ_MA_1
    *   If the Monitor Mode is INIT, the Alarm Control shall be set
    *   to Off.
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=115 
    */
  pub open spec fn initialize_REQ_MA_1(
    lastCmd: Isolette_Data_Model::On_Off,
    api_alarm_control: Isolette_Data_Model::On_Off) -> bool
  {
    (api_alarm_control == Isolette_Data_Model::On_Off::Off) &&
      (lastCmd == Isolette_Data_Model::On_Off::Off)
  }

  /** compute assume Figure_A_7
    *   Unordered bounds make REQ-MA-2 and REQ-MA-3 contradictory
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=115 
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_spec_Figure_A_7_assume(
    api_lower_alarm_temp: Isolette_Data_Model::Temp_i,
    api_monitor_mode: Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode,
    api_upper_alarm_temp: Isolette_Data_Model::Temp_i) -> bool
  {
    (api_monitor_mode == Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode::Normal_Monitor_Mode) ==>
      (api_lower_alarm_temp.degrees <= api_upper_alarm_temp.degrees)
  }

  /** compute assume Table_A_12_LowerAlarmTemp
    *   Range [96..101]
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=112 
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_spec_Table_A_12_LowerAlarmTemp_assume(
    api_lower_alarm_temp: Isolette_Data_Model::Temp_i,
    api_monitor_mode: Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode) -> bool
  {
    (api_monitor_mode == Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode::Normal_Monitor_Mode) ==>
      GUMBO_Library::Allowed_LowerAlarmTemp_spec(api_lower_alarm_temp.degrees)
  }

  /** compute assume Table_A_12_UpperAlarmTemp
    *   Range [97..102]
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=112 
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_spec_Table_A_12_UpperAlarmTemp_assume(
    api_monitor_mode: Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode,
    api_upper_alarm_temp: Isolette_Data_Model::Temp_i) -> bool
  {
    (api_monitor_mode == Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode::Normal_Monitor_Mode) ==>
      GUMBO_Library::Allowed_UpperAlarmTemp_spec(api_upper_alarm_temp.degrees)
  }

  /** compute case REQ_MA_1
    *   If the Monitor Mode is INIT, the Alarm Control shall be set
    *   to Off.
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=115 
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_case_REQ_MA_1(
    lastCmd: Isolette_Data_Model::On_Off,
    api_monitor_mode: Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode,
    api_alarm_control: Isolette_Data_Model::On_Off) -> bool
  {
    (api_monitor_mode == Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode::Init_Monitor_Mode)
    ==> ((api_alarm_control == Isolette_Data_Model::On_Off::Off) &&
           (lastCmd == Isolette_Data_Model::On_Off::Off))
  }

  /** compute case REQ_MA_2
    *   If the Monitor Mode is NORMAL and the Current Temperature is
    *   less than the Lower Alarm Temperature or greater than the Upper Alarm
    *   Temperature, the Alarm Control shall be set to On.
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=115 
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_case_REQ_MA_2(
    lastCmd: Isolette_Data_Model::On_Off,
    api_current_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i,
    api_lower_alarm_temp: Isolette_Data_Model::Temp_i,
    api_monitor_mode: Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode,
    api_upper_alarm_temp: Isolette_Data_Model::Temp_i,
    api_alarm_control: Isolette_Data_Model::On_Off) -> bool
  {
    ((api_monitor_mode == Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode::Normal_Monitor_Mode) &&
      ((api_current_tempWstatus.degrees < api_lower_alarm_temp.degrees) ||
        (api_current_tempWstatus.degrees > api_upper_alarm_temp.degrees)))
    ==> ((api_alarm_control == Isolette_Data_Model::On_Off::Onn) &&
           (lastCmd == Isolette_Data_Model::On_Off::Onn))
  }

  /** compute case REQ_MA_3
    *   If the Monitor Mode is NORMAL and the Current Temperature
    *   is greater than or equal to the Lower Alarm Temperature and less than
    *   the Lower Alarm Temperature +0.5 degrees, or the Current Temperature is
    *   greater than the Upper Alarm Temperature -0.5 degrees and less than or equal
    *   to the Upper Alarm Temperature, the value of the Alarm Control shall
    *   not be changed.
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=115 
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_case_REQ_MA_3(
    In_lastCmd: Isolette_Data_Model::On_Off,
    lastCmd: Isolette_Data_Model::On_Off,
    api_current_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i,
    api_lower_alarm_temp: Isolette_Data_Model::Temp_i,
    api_monitor_mode: Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode,
    api_upper_alarm_temp: Isolette_Data_Model::Temp_i,
    api_alarm_control: Isolette_Data_Model::On_Off) -> bool
  {
    ((api_monitor_mode == Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode::Normal_Monitor_Mode) &&
      ((api_current_tempWstatus.degrees >= api_lower_alarm_temp.degrees) &&
        (api_current_tempWstatus.degrees < api_lower_alarm_temp.degrees + 1i32) ||
        (api_current_tempWstatus.degrees > api_upper_alarm_temp.degrees - 1i32) &&
          (api_current_tempWstatus.degrees <= api_upper_alarm_temp.degrees)))
    ==> ((api_alarm_control == In_lastCmd) &&
           (lastCmd == In_lastCmd))
  }

  /** compute case REQ_MA_4
    *   If the Monitor Mode is NORMAL and the value of the Current
    *   Temperature is greater than or equal to the Lower Alarm Temperature
    *   +0.5 degrees and less than or equal to the Upper Alarm Temperature
    *   -0.5 degrees, the Alarm Control shall be set to Off.
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=115 
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_case_REQ_MA_4(
    lastCmd: Isolette_Data_Model::On_Off,
    api_current_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i,
    api_lower_alarm_temp: Isolette_Data_Model::Temp_i,
    api_monitor_mode: Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode,
    api_upper_alarm_temp: Isolette_Data_Model::Temp_i,
    api_alarm_control: Isolette_Data_Model::On_Off) -> bool
  {
    ((api_monitor_mode == Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode::Normal_Monitor_Mode) &&
      ((api_current_tempWstatus.degrees >= api_lower_alarm_temp.degrees + 1i32) &&
        (api_current_tempWstatus.degrees <= api_upper_alarm_temp.degrees - 1i32)))
    ==> ((api_alarm_control == Isolette_Data_Model::On_Off::Off) &&
           (lastCmd == Isolette_Data_Model::On_Off::Off))
  }

  /** compute case REQ_MA_5
    *   If the Monitor Mode is FAILED, the Alarm Control shall be
    *   set to On.
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=116 
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_case_REQ_MA_5(
    lastCmd: Isolette_Data_Model::On_Off,
    api_monitor_mode: Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode,
    api_alarm_control: Isolette_Data_Model::On_Off) -> bool
  {
    (api_monitor_mode == Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode::Failed_Monitor_Mode)
    ==> ((api_alarm_control == Isolette_Data_Model::On_Off::Onn) &&
           (lastCmd == Isolette_Data_Model::On_Off::Onn))
  }
}

// -- Isolette_Single_Sensor_Instance.thermostat.mt.mmm.mmm --
pub mod mmm {
  use super::*;

  // ---- GUMBO subclause functions ----

  pub open spec fn monitor_status(
    interface_failure: Isolette_Data_Model::Failure_Flag_i,
    internal_failure: Isolette_Data_Model::Failure_Flag_i,
    current_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i) -> bool
  {
    !(interface_failure.flag || internal_failure.flag) &&
      (current_tempWstatus.status == Isolette_Data_Model::ValueStatus::Valid)
  }

  pub open spec fn timeout_condition_satisfied() -> bool
  {
    false
  }

  /** initialize guarantee REQ_MMM_1
    *   Upon the first dispatch of the thread, the monitor mode is Init.
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=114 
    */
  pub open spec fn initialize_REQ_MMM_1(
    api_monitor_mode: Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode) -> bool
  {
    api_monitor_mode == Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode::Init_Monitor_Mode
  }

  /** initialize guarantee update_lastMonitorMode
    */
  pub open spec fn initialize_update_lastMonitorMode(
    lastMonitorMode: Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode,
    api_monitor_mode: Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode) -> bool
  {
    lastMonitorMode == api_monitor_mode
  }

  /** compute case REQ_MMM_2
    *   If the current mode is Init, then
    *   the mode is set to NORMAL iff the monitor status is true (valid) (see Table A-15), i.e.,
    *   if  NOT (Monitor Interface Failure OR Monitor Internal Failure)
    *   AND Current Temperature.Status = Valid.
    *   Formalized as an iff per the requirement text (with the Init timeout
    *   transition of REQ-MMM-4 excluded so the two cases cannot conflict);
    *   the converse direction is needed at the system level to conclude
    *   that NORMAL mode implies no monitor interface failure.
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=114 
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_case_REQ_MMM_2(
    lastMonitorMode: Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode,
    api_current_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i,
    api_interface_failure: Isolette_Data_Model::Failure_Flag_i,
    api_internal_failure: Isolette_Data_Model::Failure_Flag_i,
    api_monitor_mode: Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode) -> bool
  {
    (lastMonitorMode == Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode::Init_Monitor_Mode)
    ==> ((monitor_status(api_interface_failure, api_internal_failure, api_current_tempWstatus) && !(timeout_condition_satisfied())) == (api_monitor_mode == Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode::Normal_Monitor_Mode))
  }

  /** compute case REQ_MMM_Maintain_Normal
    *   'maintaining NORMAL, NORMAL to NORMAL'
    *   If the current monitor mode is Normal and the monitor status is true
    *   (no interface/internal failure and the current temperature is valid),
    *   the monitor mode stays Normal.
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=114 
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_case_REQ_MMM_Maintain_Normal(
    lastMonitorMode: Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode,
    api_current_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i,
    api_interface_failure: Isolette_Data_Model::Failure_Flag_i,
    api_internal_failure: Isolette_Data_Model::Failure_Flag_i,
    api_monitor_mode: Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode) -> bool
  {
    ((lastMonitorMode == Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode::Normal_Monitor_Mode) &&
      monitor_status(api_interface_failure, api_internal_failure, api_current_tempWstatus))
    ==> (api_monitor_mode == Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode::Normal_Monitor_Mode)
  }

  /** compute case REQ_MMM_3
    *   If the current Monitor mode is Normal, then
    *   the Monitor mode is set to Failed iff
    *   the Monitor status is false, i.e.,
    *   if  (Monitor Interface Failure OR Monitor Internal Failure)
    *   OR NOT(Current Temperature.Status = Valid)
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=114 
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_case_REQ_MMM_3(
    lastMonitorMode: Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode,
    api_current_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i,
    api_interface_failure: Isolette_Data_Model::Failure_Flag_i,
    api_internal_failure: Isolette_Data_Model::Failure_Flag_i,
    api_monitor_mode: Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode) -> bool
  {
    (lastMonitorMode == Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode::Normal_Monitor_Mode)
    ==> (!(monitor_status(api_interface_failure, api_internal_failure, api_current_tempWstatus)) == (api_monitor_mode == Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode::Failed_Monitor_Mode))
  }

  /** compute case REQ_MMM_4
    *   If the current mode is Init, then
    *   the mode is set to Failed iff the time during
    *   which the thread has been in Init mode exceeds the
    *   Monitor Init Timeout value.
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=114 
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_case_REQ_MMM_4(
    lastMonitorMode: Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode,
    api_monitor_mode: Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode) -> bool
  {
    (lastMonitorMode == Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode::Init_Monitor_Mode)
    ==> (timeout_condition_satisfied() == (api_monitor_mode == Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode::Failed_Monitor_Mode))
  }

  /** compute case Failed_Mode_Absorbing
    *   If the current mode is Failed, the mode remains Failed.
    *   Derived requirement -- not numbered in the requirements spec, but
    *   implied by Figure A-6: the monitor mode state machine has no
    *   transitions out of the Failed mode.  The diagram's implicit frame
    *   (no arc drawn means no transition) must be stated explicitly here:
    *   a contract that is silent for lastMonitorMode == Failed allows any
    *   mode value (e.g., Failed to Normal while the interface is still
    *   failing), which would break the system-level property that NORMAL
    *   mode implies no monitor interface failure.
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=114 
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_case_Failed_Mode_Absorbing(
    lastMonitorMode: Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode,
    api_monitor_mode: Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode) -> bool
  {
    (lastMonitorMode == Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode::Failed_Monitor_Mode)
    ==> (api_monitor_mode == Isolette_Data_Model::Monitor_Mode::Failed_Monitor_Mode)
  }
}

// -- Isolette_Single_Sensor_Instance.operator_interface.oip.oit --
pub mod oi {
  use super::*;

  // ---- GUMBO subclause functions ----

  pub open spec fn Allowed_UpperAlarmTempWStatus(upper: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i) -> bool
  {
    GUMBO_Library::Allowed_UpperAlarmTempWStatus_spec(upper)
  }

  /** compute guarantee Allowed_AlarmTempWStatus_Ranges
    *   An integration constraint can only refer to a single port, so need a general requires
    *   clause to relate the lower and upper temps
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_spec_Allowed_AlarmTempWStatus_Ranges_guarantee(
    api_lower_alarm_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i,
    api_upper_alarm_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i) -> bool
  {
    GUMBO_Library::Allowed_AlarmTempWStatus_Ranges_spec(api_lower_alarm_tempWstatus, api_upper_alarm_tempWstatus)
  }

  /** compute guarantee Desired_Temps_Ordered
    *   The operator interface only emits desired temperatures where the lower
    *   bound does not exceed the upper bound (establishes the system property
    *   lower_is_not_higher_than_upper consumed by the regulator subsystem)
    */
  pub open spec fn compute_spec_Desired_Temps_Ordered_guarantee(
    api_lower_desired_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i,
    api_upper_desired_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i) -> bool
  {
    api_lower_desired_tempWstatus.degrees <= api_upper_desired_tempWstatus.degrees
  }

  /** integration guarantee Allowed_LowerAlarmTempWstatus (I-Guar, outgoing port lower_alarm_tempWstatus)
    *   Table_A_12_LowerAlarmTemp: Range [96..101]:
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=112 
    */
  pub open spec fn integration_spec_Allowed_LowerAlarmTempWstatus_guarantee(
    api_lower_alarm_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i) -> bool
  {
    GUMBO_Library::isValidTempWstatus_spec(api_lower_alarm_tempWstatus) ==>
      ((96i32 <= api_lower_alarm_tempWstatus.degrees) &&
        (api_lower_alarm_tempWstatus.degrees <= 101i32))
  }

  /** integration guarantee Allowed_UpperAlarmTempWstatus (I-Guar, outgoing port upper_alarm_tempWstatus)
    *   Table_A_12_UpperAlarmTemp: Range [97..102]
    *   https://www.faa.gov/sites/faa.gov/files/aircraft/air_cert/design_approvals/air_software/AR-08-32.pdf#page=112 
    */
  pub open spec fn integration_spec_Allowed_UpperAlarmTempWstatus_guarantee(
    api_upper_alarm_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i) -> bool
  {
    Allowed_UpperAlarmTempWStatus(api_upper_alarm_tempWstatus)
  }
}

} // verus!

// Do not edit this file as it will be overwritten if HAMR codegen is rerun

//! Write frames -- per component, the global frame constrains every
//! SystemState field outside the component's write set to be unchanged
//! across its firing; the local frame is `true`.

#![allow(non_snake_case)]

use vstd::prelude::*;

use crate::system_state::SystemState;

verus! {

/** MRI writes: upper_desired_temp, lower_desired_temp, displayed_temp, regulator_status, reg_interface_failure.
  * Everything else must be unchanged.
  */
pub open spec fn mri_global_write_frame(pre: SystemState, post: SystemState) -> bool
{
  pre.heat_control == post.heat_control
  && pre.lastCmd == post.lastCmd
  && pre.regulator_mode == post.regulator_mode

// Do not edit this file as it will be overwritten if HAMR codegen is rerun

//! Commutativity VCs (execIndependent), one per component-component MHIP
//! pair. Assertion-free state equalities over the action abstractions --
//! shared by all of the composition's properties.
//! Each proof fn has an empty body: Verus discharges `requires ==> ensures`
//! via SMT; add proof hints in the body if a VC does not discharge.

#![allow(non_snake_case)]
#![allow(unused_variables)]

use vstd::prelude::*;

use crate::actions::*;
use crate::system_state::SystemState;

verus! {

/** VC[0]: Commutativity (execIndependent) -- firing OI then TS
  * yields the same state as TS then OI (MHIP pair t0/t1).
  * Discharges by congruence when the write sets are disjoint from each
  * other's write sets and read scopes. */
pub proof fn vc_commutativity_oi_ts(st: SystemState, mid_a: SystemState, post_a: SystemState, mid_b: SystemState, post_b: SystemState)
  requires
    oi_fire(st, mid_a),
    ts_fire(mid_a, post_a),
    ts_fire(st, mid_b),
    oi_fire(mid_b, post_b),
  ensures
    post_a == post_b,
{}

/** VC[1]: Commutativity (execIndependent) -- firing DRF then DMF
  * yields the same state as DMF then DRF (MHIP pair t4/t8).
  * Discharges by congruence when the write sets are disjoint from each
  * other's write sets and read scopes. */
pub proof fn vc_commutativity_drf_dmf(st: SystemState, mid_a: SystemState, post_a: SystemState, mid_b: SystemState, post_b: SystemState)
  requires
    drf_fire(st, mid_a),
    dmf_fire(mid_a, post_a),
    dmf_fire(st, mid_b),
    drf_fire(mid_b, post_b),
  ensures
    post_a == post_b,
{}

/** VC[2]: Commutativity (execIndependent) -- firing MRI then DMF
  * yields the same state as DMF then MRI (MHIP pair t5/t8).
  * Discharges by congruence when the write sets are disjoint from each
  * other's write sets and read scopes. */
pub proof fn vc_commutativity_mri_dmf(st: SystemState, mid_a: SystemState, post_a: SystemState, mid_b: SystemState, post_b: SystemState)
  requires
    mri_fire(st, mid_a),
    dmf_fire(mid_a, post_a),
    dmf_fire(st, mid_b),
    mri_fire(mid_b, post_b),
  ensures
    post_a == post_b,
{}

/** VC[3]: Commutativity (execIndependent) -- firing DRF then MMI
  * yields the same state as MMI then DRF (MHIP pair t4/t9).
  * Discharges by congruence when the write sets are disjoint from each
  * other's write sets and read scopes. */
pub proof fn vc_commutativity_drf_mmi(st: SystemState, mid_a: SystemState, post_a: SystemState, mid_b: SystemState, post_b: SystemState)
  requires
    drf_fire(st, mid_a),
    mmi_fire(mid_a, post_a),
    mmi_fire(st, mid_b),
    drf_fire(mid_b, post_b),
  ensures
    post_a == post_b,
{}

/** VC[4]: Commutativity (execIndependent) -- firing MRM then DMF
  * yields the same state as DMF then MRM (MHIP pair t6/t8).
  * Discharges by congruence when the write sets are disjoint from each

{}

# Do not edit this file as it will be overwritten if HAMR codegen is rerun

# `make all` (or `make`) verifies the whole crate -- every property plus the
# shared, property-independent Commutativity VCs -- in one `cargo-verus
# verify`. `make <property>` runs `cargo-verus verify` on just that
# property's generated VCs (its vc_init / vc_sequential / vc_post_pre /
# vc_independence modules).

ENV_VARS = RUSTC_BOOTSTRAP=1

CARGO_FLAGS = -Z build-std=core,alloc,compiler_builtins \
              -Z build-std-features=compiler-builtins-mem \
              --target aarch64-unknown-none

# verify the entire crate (every property plus the shared Commutativity VCs)
.PHONY: all
all:
	$(ENV_VARS) cargo-verus verify $(CARGO_FLAGS)

# Compile every crate WITHOUT verification so the dependency crates (vstd,
# data, the GUMBO library crates) are cached as Verus import libraries before
# any per-property `--verify-module` run -- that flag is otherwise forwarded
# to the dependency crates too and fails on a clean build (see header note).
.PHONY: warm-deps
warm-deps:
	$(ENV_VARS) cargo-verus verify $(CARGO_FLAGS) -- --no-verify

# verify only property 'normal_mode_heat'
.PHONY: normal_mode_heat
normal_mode_heat: warm-deps
	$(ENV_VARS) cargo-verus verify $(CARGO_FLAGS) -- --verify-module normal_mode_heat::vc_init --verify-module normal_mode_heat::vc_sequential --verify-module normal_mode_heat::vc_post_pre --verify-module normal_mode_heat::vc_independence

.PHONY: normal_mode_heat_off
normal_mode_heat_off: warm-deps

# Do not edit this file as it will be overwritten if HAMR codegen is rerun

export TOP_DIR := $(abspath $(dir ${MAKEFILE_LIST}))
export TOP_BUILD_DIR := $(abspath build)

# Optional configuration file. Usage: make CONFIG=monitor.mk
# Variables defined with ?= below (e.g. MICROKIT_CONFIG) can be
# overridden by exporting them from the config file.
ifdef CONFIG
include $(CONFIG)
endif

override MICROKIT_SDK := $(abspath ${MICROKIT_SDK})

# SYSTEM_MAKEFILE points to the makefile containing all compile/link rules.
# Override this only if you want to substitute an entirely different rule set
SYSTEM_MAKEFILE ?= system.mk

# By default we make a debug build so that the client debug prints can be seen.
export MICROKIT_CONFIG ?= debug

export CPU = cortex-a53
export QEMU = qemu-system-aarch64

export AR := ar
export CC := clang
export DTC := dtc
export LD := ld.lld
export RANLIB := llvm-ranlib

export CRATES_DIR := $(TOP_DIR)/crates

export TARGET := aarch64-none-elf

export MICROKIT_SDK := $(abspath $(MICROKIT_SDK))
export MICROKIT_TOOL := $(abspath $(MICROKIT_SDK)/bin/microkit)
export MICROKIT_BOARD_DIR := $(abspath $(MICROKIT_SDK)/board/$(MICROKIT_BOARD)/$(MICROKIT_CONFIG))

IMAGE_FILE := $(TOP_BUILD_DIR)/loader.img
REPORT_FILE := $(TOP_BUILD_DIR)/report.txt

# By default, cargo-verus is used to build Rust crates, and it fails if verification does not succeed.
# To skip verification, set the RUST_MAKE_TARGET environment variable to use a cargo build target.
# Example:
#
#   RUST_MAKE_TARGET=build-release make

all: ${IMAGE_FILE}

.PHONY: check_microkit
check_microkit:
	@if [ -z "$(strip $(MICROKIT_BOARD))" ]; then \
		echo "MICROKIT_BOARD must be specified"; \
		exit 1; \
	fi
	@if [ -z "$(strip $(MICROKIT_SDK))" ]; then \
		echo "MICROKIT_SDK must be specified"; \
		exit 1; \
	fi

${IMAGE_FILE}: check_microkit ${TOP_BUILD_DIR}/Makefile FORCE
	${MAKE} -C ${TOP_BUILD_DIR} $(notdir $@)

qemu ${REPORT_FILE}: $(IMAGE_FILE)
	${MAKE} -C ${TOP_BUILD_DIR} $(notdir $@)

clean:: ${TOP_BUILD_DIR}/Makefile
	${MAKE} -C ${TOP_BUILD_DIR} clean

clobber:: clean
	rm -rf $(TOP_DIR)/build

${TOP_BUILD_DIR}/Makefile: $(SYSTEM_MAKEFILE)
	mkdir -p ${TOP_BUILD_DIR}
	cp $(SYSTEM_MAKEFILE) ${TOP_BUILD_DIR}/Makefile

test:: ${TOP_BUILD_DIR}/Makefile
	${MAKE} -C ${TOP_BUILD_DIR} test

verus: ${TOP_BUILD_DIR}/Makefile
	${MAKE} -C ${TOP_BUILD_DIR} verus
	${MAKE} -C ${TOP_BUILD_DIR} verus-sys-proof

FORCE:

	$(MICROKIT_TOOL) $(TOP_BUILD_DIR)/$(MSD).merged --search-path $(TOP_BUILD_DIR) --board $(MICROKIT_BOARD) --config $(MICROKIT_CONFIG) -o $(IMAGE_FILE) -r $(REPORT_FILE)

// Do not edit this file as it will be overwritten if HAMR codegen is rerun

pub type sb_event_counter_t = usize;

pub type microkit_channel = u32;

# This file will not be overwritten if HAMR codegen is rerun

[package]
name = "data"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"

[dependencies]
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Verus crate dependencies
#
# It is recommended to use the Verus release:
# https://github.com/verus-lang/verus/releases/tag/release/0.2026.01.23.1650a05
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
vstd = { version = "=0.0.0-2026-01-25-0057", default-features=false }
verus_builtin = { version = "=0.0.0-2026-01-25-0057" }
verus_builtin_macros = { version = "=0.0.0-2026-01-25-0057" }

[package.metadata.verus]
verify = true

# This file will not be overwritten if HAMR codegen is rerun

# Verus hooks into rustc internals (rustc-dev, rustc_driver) which change
# nightly-to-nightly with no stability guarantees. The nightly date is pinned
# to match the Verus crate versions in Cargo.toml -- using a later nightly may
# cause compilation failures. If you update the Verus dependencies, update the
# nightly date to match.
# The rustc-dev and rust-src components are required for Verus verification;
# llvm-tools-preview is needed for microkit's no_std linking;
# rust-analyzer provides IDE support (code completion, type hints, etc.).

[toolchain]
channel = "nightly-2026-01-25"
components = [ "rustfmt", "rust-src", "rustc-dev", "llvm-tools-preview", "rust-analyzer" ]

// This file will not be overwritten if HAMR codegen is rerun

pub mod thermostat_rt_mri_mri_app;

# This file will not be overwritten if HAMR codegen is rerun

[package]
name = "thermostat_rt_mri_mri"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"

[dependencies]
data = { path = "../data" }
linux-raw-sys = { version = "=0.12.0", default-features = false }
log = "0.4.28"
GUMBO_Library = { path = "../GUMBO_Library" }

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# seL4 Rust crate dependencies
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
sel4 = { git = "https://github.com/seL4/rust-sel4", features = ["single-threaded"], optional = true, tag="v2.0.0" }
sel4-logging = { git = "https://github.com/seL4/rust-sel4", optional = true, tag="v2.0.0" }

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Verus crate dependencies
#
# It is recommended to use the Verus release:
# https://github.com/verus-lang/verus/releases/tag/release/0.2026.01.23.1650a05
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
vstd = { version = "=0.0.0-2026-01-25-0057", default-features=false }
verus_builtin = { version = "=0.0.0-2026-01-25-0057" }
verus_builtin_macros = { version = "=0.0.0-2026-01-25-0057" }


[dev-dependencies]
lazy_static = "1.5.0"
once_cell = "1.21.3"
serial_test = "3.2.0"
proptest = "1.8.0"
env_logger = "0.11.8"

[lib]
path = "src/lib.rs"
crate-type = ["staticlib"]

[features]
sel4 = ["dep:sel4", "dep:sel4-logging" ]

[package.metadata.verus]
verify = true

# This file will not be overwritten if HAMR codegen is rerun

microkit_sdk_config_dir := $(MICROKIT_SDK)/board/$(MICROKIT_BOARD)/$(MICROKIT_CONFIG)

sel4_include_dirs := $(firstword $(wildcard $(microkit_sdk_config_dir)/include \
                                            $(microkit_sdk_config_dir)/debug/include))

ENV_VARS = RUSTC_BOOTSTRAP=1

BUILD_ENV_VARS = $(ENV_VARS) \
                 SEL4_INCLUDE_DIRS=$(abspath $(sel4_include_dirs))

CARGO_FLAGS = -Z build-std=core,alloc,compiler_builtins \
              -Z build-std-features=compiler-builtins-mem \
              --target aarch64-unknown-none

all: build-verus-release

build-verus-release:
	$(BUILD_ENV_VARS) cargo-verus build --features sel4 $(CARGO_FLAGS) --release

build-verus:
	$(BUILD_ENV_VARS) cargo-verus build --features sel4 $(CARGO_FLAGS)

build-release:
	$(BUILD_ENV_VARS) cargo build --features sel4 $(CARGO_FLAGS) --release

build:
	$(BUILD_ENV_VARS) cargo build --features sel4 $(CARGO_FLAGS)

verus:
	$(ENV_VARS) cargo-verus verify $(CARGO_FLAGS)

verus-json:
	$(ENV_VARS) cargo-verus verify $(CARGO_FLAGS) -- --output-json --time > verus_results.json

# Test Example:
#   Run all unit tests
#   Usage: make test
#
#   Run only unit tests whose name contains 'proptest'
#   Usage: make test args=proptest

test-release:
	cargo test $(args) --release

test:
	cargo test $(args)

# Coverage Example:
#   Generate a test coverage report combining the results of all unit tests
#   Usage: make coverage
#
#   Generate a test coverage report for unit tests whose name contains 'proptest'
#   Usage: make coverage args=proptest

coverage:
	cargo install grcov
	@exists=0; if [ -f target/coverage/report/index.html ]; then exists=1; fi; \
	rm -rf target/coverage; \
	CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 RUSTFLAGS='-Cinstrument-coverage' LLVM_PROFILE_FILE='target/coverage/cargo-test-%p-%m.profraw' \
	cargo test $(args); \
	grcov . --binary-path ./target/debug/deps/ -s . -t html --branch --ignore-not-existing -o target/coverage/report; \
	if [ $$exists -eq 0 ]; then open target/coverage/report/index.html; fi

clean:
	cargo clean

# This file will not be overwritten if HAMR codegen is rerun

# Verus hooks into rustc internals (rustc-dev, rustc_driver) which change

# This file will not be overwritten if HAMR codegen is rerun

all: build-verus-release

build-verus-release:
	$(BUILD_ENV_VARS) cargo-verus build --features sel4 $(CARGO_FLAGS) --release

build-verus:
	$(BUILD_ENV_VARS) cargo-verus build --features sel4 $(CARGO_FLAGS)

build-release:
	$(BUILD_ENV_VARS) cargo build --features sel4 $(CARGO_FLAGS) --release

build:
	$(BUILD_ENV_VARS) cargo build --features sel4 $(CARGO_FLAGS)

verus:
	$(ENV_VARS) cargo-verus verify $(CARGO_FLAGS)

verus-json:
	$(ENV_VARS) cargo-verus verify $(CARGO_FLAGS) -- --output-json --time > verus_results.json

# Test Example:

# Coverage Example:

#   Usage: make coverage
#
#   Generate a test coverage report for unit tests whose name contains 'proptest'
#   Usage: make coverage args=proptest

coverage:
	cargo install grcov
	@exists=0; if [ -f target/coverage/report/index.html ]; then exists=1; fi; \
	rm -rf target/coverage; \
	CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 RUSTFLAGS='-Cinstrument-coverage' LLVM_PROFILE_FILE='target/coverage/cargo-test-%p-%m.profraw' \
	cargo test $(args); \
	grcov . --binary-path ./target/debug/deps/ -s . -t html --branch --ignore-not-existing -o target/coverage/report; \
	if [ $$exists -eq 0 ]; then open target/coverage/report/index.html; fi

clean:
	cargo clean

sel4 = { git = "https://github.com/seL4/rust-sel4", features = ["single-threaded"], optional = true, tag="v2.0.0" }
sel4-logging = { git = "https://github.com/seL4/rust-sel4", optional = true, tag="v2.0.0" }

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Verus crate dependencies
#
# It is recommended to use the Verus release:
# https://github.com/verus-lang/verus/releases/tag/release/0.2026.01.23.1650a05
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
vstd = { version = "=0.0.0-2026-01-25-0057", default-features=false }
verus_builtin = { version = "=0.0.0-2026-01-25-0057" }
verus_builtin_macros = { version = "=0.0.0-2026-01-25-0057" }


[dev-dependencies]
lazy_static = "1.5.0"
once_cell = "1.21.3"
serial_test = "3.2.0"
proptest = "1.8.0"
env_logger = "0.11.8"

[lib]
path = "src/lib.rs"
crate-type = ["staticlib"]

[features]
sel4 = ["dep:sel4", "dep:sel4-logging" ]

[package.metadata.verus]
verify = true

# This file will not be overwritten if HAMR codegen is rerun

microkit_sdk_config_dir := $(MICROKIT_SDK)/board/$(MICROKIT_BOARD)/$(MICROKIT_CONFIG)

sel4_include_dirs := $(firstword $(wildcard $(microkit_sdk_config_dir)/include \
                                            $(microkit_sdk_config_dir)/debug/include))

ENV_VARS = RUSTC_BOOTSTRAP=1

BUILD_ENV_VARS = $(ENV_VARS) \
                 SEL4_INCLUDE_DIRS=$(abspath $(sel4_include_dirs))

CARGO_FLAGS = -Z build-std=core,alloc,compiler_builtins \
              -Z build-std-features=compiler-builtins-mem \
              --target aarch64-unknown-none

all: build-verus-release

build-verus-release:
	$(BUILD_ENV_VARS) cargo-verus build --features sel4 $(CARGO_FLAGS) --release

build-verus:
	$(BUILD_ENV_VARS) cargo-verus build --features sel4 $(CARGO_FLAGS)

build-release:
	$(BUILD_ENV_VARS) cargo build --features sel4 $(CARGO_FLAGS) --release

build:
	$(BUILD_ENV_VARS) cargo build --features sel4 $(CARGO_FLAGS)

verus:
	$(ENV_VARS) cargo-verus verify $(CARGO_FLAGS)

verus-json:
	$(ENV_VARS) cargo-verus verify $(CARGO_FLAGS) -- --output-json --time > verus_results.json

# Test Example:
#   Run all unit tests
#   Usage: make test
#
#   Run only unit tests whose name contains 'proptest'
#   Usage: make test args=proptest

test-release:
	cargo test $(args) --release

test:
	cargo test $(args)

# Coverage Example:
#   Generate a test coverage report combining the results of all unit tests
#   Usage: make coverage
#
#   Generate a test coverage report for unit tests whose name contains 'proptest'
#   Usage: make coverage args=proptest

coverage:
	cargo install grcov
	@exists=0; if [ -f target/coverage/report/index.html ]; then exists=1; fi; \
	rm -rf target/coverage; \
	CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 RUSTFLAGS='-Cinstrument-coverage' LLVM_PROFILE_FILE='target/coverage/cargo-test-%p-%m.profraw' \
	cargo test $(args); \
	grcov . --binary-path ./target/debug/deps/ -s . -t html --branch --ignore-not-existing -o target/coverage/report; \
	if [ $$exists -eq 0 ]; then open target/coverage/report/index.html; fi

clean:
	cargo clean

// Do not edit this file as it will be overwritten if HAMR codegen is rerun

use data::*;

macro_rules! implies {
  ($lhs: expr, $rhs: expr) => {
    !$lhs || $rhs
  };
}

macro_rules! impliesL {
  ($lhs: expr, $rhs: expr) => {
    !$lhs | $rhs
  };
}

pub fn ROUND(num: i32) -> i32
{
  num
}

/** Initialize EntryPointContract
  *
  * guarantee RegulatorStatusIsInitiallyInit
  * @param api_regulator_status outgoing data port
  */
pub fn initialize_RegulatorStatusIsInitiallyInit(api_regulator_status: Isolette_Data_Model::Status) -> bool
{
  api_regulator_status == Isolette_Data_Model::Status::Init_Status
}

/** IEP-Guar: Initialize Entrypoint for mri
  *
  * @param api_displayed_temp outgoing data port
  * @param api_interface_failure outgoing data port
  * @param api_lower_desired_temp outgoing data port
  * @param api_regulator_status outgoing data port
  * @param api_upper_desired_temp outgoing data port
  */
pub fn initialize_IEP_Guar(
  api_displayed_temp: Isolette_Data_Model::Temp_i,
  api_interface_failure: Isolette_Data_Model::Failure_Flag_i,
  api_lower_desired_temp: Isolette_Data_Model::Temp_i,
  api_regulator_status: Isolette_Data_Model::Status,
  api_upper_desired_temp: Isolette_Data_Model::Temp_i) -> bool
{
  initialize_RegulatorStatusIsInitiallyInit(api_regulator_status)
}

/** IEP-Post: Initialize Entrypoint Post-Condition
  *
  * @param api_displayed_temp outgoing data port
  * @param api_interface_failure outgoing data port
  * @param api_lower_desired_temp outgoing data port
  * @param api_regulator_status outgoing data port
  * @param api_upper_desired_temp outgoing data port
  */
pub fn initialize_IEP_Post(
  api_displayed_temp: Isolette_Data_Model::Temp_i,
  api_interface_failure: Isolette_Data_Model::Failure_Flag_i,
  api_lower_desired_temp: Isolette_Data_Model::Temp_i,
  api_regulator_status: Isolette_Data_Model::Status,
  api_upper_desired_temp: Isolette_Data_Model::Temp_i) -> bool
{
  initialize_IEP_Guar(api_displayed_temp, api_interface_failure, api_lower_desired_temp, api_regulator_status, api_upper_desired_temp)
}

/** Compute Entrypoint Contract
  *
  * assumes lower_is_not_higher_than_upper
  *   The ordering of the desired temperature range is only a meaningful
  *   constraint on Valid readings
  * @param api_lower_desired_tempWstatus incoming data port
  * @param api_upper_desired_tempWstatus incoming data port
  */
pub fn compute_spec_lower_is_not_higher_than_upper_assume(
  api_lower_desired_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i,
  api_upper_desired_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i) -> bool
{
  impliesL!(
    (api_lower_desired_tempWstatus.status == Isolette_Data_Model::ValueStatus::Valid) &
      (api_upper_desired_tempWstatus.status == Isolette_Data_Model::ValueStatus::Valid),
    (api_lower_desired_tempWstatus.degrees <= api_upper_desired_tempWstatus.degrees))
}

/** CEP-T-Assm: Top-level assume contracts for mri's compute entrypoint
  *
  * @param api_lower_desired_tempWstatus incoming data port
  * @param api_upper_desired_tempWstatus incoming data port
  */
pub fn compute_CEP_T_Assm(
  api_lower_desired_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i,
  api_upper_desired_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i) -> bool
{
  let r0: bool = compute_spec_lower_is_not_higher_than_upper_assume(api_lower_desired_tempWstatus, api_upper_desired_tempWstatus);

  return r0;
}

/** CEP-Pre: Compute Entrypoint Pre-Condition for mri
  *
  * @param api_current_tempWstatus incoming data port
  * @param api_lower_desired_tempWstatus incoming data port
  * @param api_regulator_mode incoming data port
  * @param api_upper_desired_tempWstatus incoming data port
  */
pub fn compute_CEP_Pre(
  api_current_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i,
  api_lower_desired_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i,
  api_regulator_mode: Isolette_Data_Model::Regulator_Mode,
  api_upper_desired_tempWstatus: Isolette_Data_Model::TempWstatus_i) -> bool
{
  // CEP-Assm: assume clauses of mri's compute entrypoint
  let r0: bool = compute_CEP_T_Assm(api_lower_desired_tempWstatus, api_upper_desired_tempWstatus);

  return r0;
}

/** Compute Entrypoint Contract
  *
  * guarantee lower_is_lower_temp
  *   Derived requirement, not in AR-08-32: MHS unconditionally assumes the
  *   Desired Range is well-ordered,.
  * @param api_lower_desired_temp outgoing data port

  let r4: bool = compute_case_REQ_MRI_5();
  let r5: bool = compute_case_REQ_MRI_6(api_upper_desired_tempWstatus, api_interface_failure);

  let r8: bool = compute_case_REQ_MRI_9(api_interface_failure);
